agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"SQL injection vulnerability in public-facing endpoint — immediate exploitation risk"},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized string interpolation","risk":"Attacker can exfiltrate, modify, or destroy the entire database through crafted customer_id or status_filter values","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries() — f-string query construction","reproduction":"GET /api/deliveries?customer_id=x' OR '1'='1' --&status_filter=anything → returns all deliveries for all customers. Destructive: customer_id=x'; DROP TABLE deliveries; -- wipes the table.","fix":"Use parameterized queries: db_conn.execute(\"SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?\", (customer_id, status_filter))","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security: no user-supplied input should reach SQL without parameterization"},{"title":"No input validation on public-facing parameters","risk":"Even with parameterized queries, unbounded or untyped inputs can cause unexpected behavior (e.g., wildcard status values, empty strings, extremely long strings causing resource exhaustion)","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries() function signature — no validation before query","reproduction":"GET /api/deliveries?customer_id=&status_filter= → returns rows matching empty strings or causes unexpected results depending on DB. GET with a 10MB customer_id string could stress memory.","fix":"Validate customer_id against expected format (e.g., UUID regex) and status_filter against an allowlist of known statuses before executing the query. Add length limits.","time_to_fix":"15 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Reliability: endpoint must reject malformed input before hitting the database"},{"title":"SELECT * exposes all columns to API consumers","risk":"Schema changes silently leak new sensitive columns (e.g., internal_notes, driver_ssn, cost_price) to the public API response without review","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries() — SELECT *","reproduction":"Add a sensitive column to the deliveries table (ALTER TABLE deliveries ADD COLUMN internal_notes TEXT). The endpoint immediately returns it to public API consumers with zero code change.","fix":"Enumerate columns explicitly: SELECT id, customer_id, status, tracking_number, created_at FROM deliveries","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security: public API must not leak internal/sensitive data"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No authorization boundary check","description":"The function trusts the caller-supplied customer_id. If the API layer doesn't enforce that the authenticated user owns that customer_id, any user can query any other customer's deliveries (IDOR).","location":"search_deliveries() — no ownership verification"},{"title":"No pagination or result limit","description":"A customer with millions of deliveries (or an attacker using the injection to return all rows) will cause unbounded memory allocation and response size, potentially OOMing the service.","location":"search_deliveries() — no LIMIT clause"}],"suggestions":["Use an ORM or query builder that enforces parameterization by default","Add LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination","Log and rate-limit queries per customer to detect abuse","Return a well-defined response DTO rather than raw row objects"],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code is actively exploitable the moment it handles public traffic. A single crafted request can compromise the database.","rollback_capability":"N/A — if deployed, damage (data exfiltration/deletion) may already be done before rollback.","backward_compatibility":"Fixing to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for legitimate callers.","monitoring_coverage":"No logging of query parameters or failed queries — attacks would be invisible.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["auth_security: SQL injection allows full DB access bypass; IDOR allows cross-customer data access"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Fix parameterization, add input validation and auth checks, then re-review."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":99,"high_confidence_areas":["SQL injection is textbook and unambiguous","SELECT * data leakage risk","Missing input validation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact DB engine (affects injection syntax slightly, but all are vulnerable)","Whether an upstream API layer adds any auth — reviewed code shows none"],"assumptions":["customer_id and status_filter reach this function unsanitized from HTTP params as stated","No WAF or SQL-aware proxy sits in front of this endpoint"]}}
